title: A Big Piece Of Garbage

jokes.text: Good news, everyone. Tomorrow, you'll make a delivery to Ebola Nine, the virus planet.
jokes.setup: Professor announces 'good news' with cheerful delivery
jokes.punchline: The 'good news' is a deadly delivery to a virus planet
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-defining joke. Farnsworth's cheerful delivery of horrific news establishes his callous disregard for his crew's safety. Excellent economy and structure.

jokes.text: -Why can't they go today? -Tonight's special.
jokes.setup: Reasonable question about scheduling
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth wants them alive for a special event, implying tomorrow's delivery will kill them
jokes.explanation: Solid follow-up that reinforces the dark implications of the first joke. Good character integration.

jokes.text: -Sounds boring. -Oh, my, yes. But not this year...
jokes.setup: Someone calls the scientific symposium boring
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth eagerly agrees it's boring but gets excited anyway
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Farnsworth's oblivious enthusiasm. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: -Sometimes it's off by seconds... what with free will and all.
jokes.setup: Death Clock claims to predict exact death time
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of 'free will' as minor variable affecting death prediction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical absurdity delivered deadpan. Free will treated as minor calibration issue is wonderfully ridiculous.

jokes.text: Dibs on his CD player!
jokes.setup: Someone learns their exact death time from the Death Clock
jokes.punchline: Immediate callous claim on their belongings
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed dark comedy. The immediate transition from death prediction to inheritance planning is brilliantly callous.

jokes.text: Look who's showing his wrinkled face.
jokes.setup: Antagonist appears at scientific gathering
jokes.punchline: Insult about age/appearance establishes rivalry
jokes.explanation: Generic insult that serves plot function but lacks wit or originality. Functional character establishment.

jokes.text: Leave science to the 120-year-olds.
jokes.setup: Telling old scientist he's too old
jokes.punchline: 120-year-olds are the 'young' generation
jokes.explanation: Good future-world absurdity. The age reversal joke works well for the sci-fi setting.

jokes.text: I invented things when you were barely turning senile.
jokes.setup: Defensive response to age insults
jokes.punchline: 'Barely turning senile' as a young age milestone
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that subverts expectations. 'Barely turning senile' as youth is perfectly absurd and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: A-minus? No one gives Ogden Wernstrom an A-minus!
jokes.setup: Student receives less than perfect grade
jokes.punchline: Dramatically outraged reaction to minor grade reduction
jokes.explanation: Classic petty villain origin. The extreme reaction to a minor slight is good character work.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, but penmanship counts.
jokes.setup: Student outraged by A-minus grade
jokes.punchline: Professor calmly cites penmanship as reason for grade reduction
jokes.explanation: Nice understated response. The mundane reason for the century-long vendetta is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: And here it is, slightly over 99 years later and still no revenge.
jokes.setup: Wernstrom swore revenge 100 years ago
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth smugly notes the revenge is overdue
jokes.explanation: Good setup for dramatic irony. Farnsworth's premature confidence sets up his later downfall.

jokes.text: I've been perusing your fortified wine list... and I've selected the '71 Hobo's Delight... the '57 Chateau Par-Tay, and the '66 Thunder Chevitz.
jokes.setup: Fancy wine selection with sophisticated language
jokes.punchline: Wine names are ridiculous: 'Hobo's Delight,' 'Chateau Par-Tay,' 'Thunder Chevitz'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment. Bender's sophisticated wine pretensions undercut by ridiculous names. Great wordplay in the wine names.

jokes.text: Exquisite choices, sir. And mix them all together in a big jug.
jokes.setup: Waiter validates sophisticated wine selection
jokes.punchline: Bender wants them mixed together in a jug, destroying any pretense
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline to the wine pretension setup. The misdirection is expertly executed and very in-character for Bender.

jokes.text: Welcome to this year's symposium. I'm your host, Ron Popeil... inventor of Mr. Microphone, the Spray-on Toupee... and the technology to keep heads alive in jars.
jokes.setup: Host introduces himself with real-world inventions
jokes.punchline: Escalates from silly real products to keeping severed heads alive
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from real Ronco products to absurd future technology. The progression feels natural yet shocking.

jokes.text: Wait, there's more.
jokes.setup: Ron Popeil's catchphrase from real infomercials
jokes.punchline: Using his actual catchphrase in this context
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration using the real Popeil's actual catchphrase. Not highly original but very satisfying for those who get the reference.

jokes.text: Fetch! [fish swims away] Now, sit. I said sit! Bad fish!
jokes.setup: Reverse Scuba Suit demonstration with trained fish
jokes.punchline: Fish doesn't obey commands like a dog would
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy. The absurdity of trying to train fish like dogs works well visually.

jokes.text: I hope it's not as lame as last year's Death Clock.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth mentioned Death Clock as his latest invention
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was actually last year's failed invention
jokes.explanation: Great callback that recontextualizes the opening. Farnsworth's 'latest' invention is actually old and failed.

jokes.text: We laughed until our teeth fell out. Come along, Cinnamon.
jokes.setup: Describing reaction to bad invention
jokes.punchline: Literally laughed until teeth fell out, then calls companion 'Cinnamon'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with the literal tooth loss. The pet name 'Cinnamon' adds character detail.

jokes.text: I have to invent something new in the next 10 minutes. Perhaps some sort of Death Clock!
jokes.setup: Desperate need for new invention under time pressure
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggests the same failed invention from last year
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Farnsworth's absent-mindedness and lack of self-awareness about his failures.

jokes.text: Pencils down, prune-face.
jokes.setup: Stopping Farnsworth's last-minute invention
jokes.punchline: Age-based insult 'prune-face'
jokes.explanation: Generic age insult that serves the plot but lacks wit. Functional character antagonism.

jokes.text: We all know telescopes allow us to see distant objects... but what if we want to smell those objects?
jokes.setup: Logical scientific premise about telescopes
jokes.punchline: Absurd extension to smelling distant objects
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic - takes scientific inquiry in a completely unnecessary but logical direction.

jokes.text: Thanks to my new invention... the Smell-O-Scope.
jokes.setup: Grandiose introduction of scientific breakthrough
jokes.punchline: It's just a telescope for smelling things
jokes.explanation: Great concept with memorable name. The portmanteau 'Smell-O-Scope' is perfectly constructed and memorable.

jokes.text: The odor travels past this coffee stain here. Around the olive pit... and into this cigar burn. And this appears to be a... doodle of myself as a cowboy.
jokes.setup: Technical explanation of how the Smell-O-Scope works
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'scientific instrument' is just a messy paper with random damage
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating visual gag. The progression from technical terms to increasingly ridiculous elements culminating in the cowboy doodle is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: [CROWD LAUGHS] Oh, my. [CROWD LAUGHS]
jokes.setup: Farnsworth's presentation is being mocked
jokes.punchline: His confused reaction to the laughter makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. Farnsworth's obliviousness to being mocked creates sympathy and humor.

jokes.text: I've waited 100 years for this. I give your invention the worst grade imaginable: An A-minus-minus!
jokes.setup: Century-long revenge plot reaches climax
jokes.punchline: Ultimate revenge is giving a grade slightly worse than A-minus
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to the setup. The petty nature of the revenge - downgrading from A-minus to A-minus-minus - is perfectly absurd and character-appropriate. Excellent callback structure.

jokes.text: Perhaps 149 is just too old to be a scientist.
jokes.setup: Character reflecting on his failure and age
jokes.punchline: 149 years old presented as possibly too old
jokes.explanation: Good world-building humor establishing the extreme longevity in this universe while maintaining relatability.

jokes.text: There were many times in my century when I never gave up. Never. Are you even listening to me? Oh, I give up.
jokes.setup: Fry giving inspiring speech about never giving up
jokes.punchline: Gives up mid-speech when ignored
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-appropriate irony. Fry's immediate contradiction of his own inspirational message is excellently structured.

jokes.text: No, I remembered that I built one last year!
jokes.setup: Fry asks if Farnsworth built the Smell-O-Scope
jokes.punchline: Didn't build it now, just remembered building it before
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reinforces Farnsworth's absent-mindedness. The deflation from triumph to memory is well-executed.

jokes.text: As long as you don't make me smell Uranus. -I don't get it.
jokes.setup: Fry makes obvious Uranus joke setup
jokes.punchline: Leela doesn't understand the juvenile pun
jokes.explanation: Sets up the bigger joke about Uranus being renamed. Functional setup rather than standalone humor.

jokes.text: Astronomers renamed Uranus in 2620 to end that stupid joke once and for all. -What's it called now? -Urectum.
jokes.setup: Planet renamed to eliminate juvenile humor
jokes.punchline: New name 'Urectum' is even worse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection and escalation. The attempt to eliminate juvenile humor by making it worse is perfectly executed and became iconic.

jokes.text: Here, let me locate it for you. No, I think I'll just smell around over here.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth offers to point the Smell-O-Scope at Urectum
jokes.punchline: Fry politely declines and moves away
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up reaction that extends the Uranus/Urectum joke naturally without overdoing it.

jokes.text: Remarkable! A stench so foul it's right off the funkometer!
jokes.setup: Fry discovers something that smells terrible
jokes.punchline: 'Off the funkometer' as measurement of extreme stench
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay with 'funkometer.' The scientific measurement of funk is appropriately absurd for Farnsworth.

jokes.text: Oh! Name it after me!
jokes.setup: Discovery of smelliest object in known universe
jokes.punchline: Bender wants the horrible thing named after him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment. His desire for fame regardless of the nature of the discovery is very in-character.

jokes.text: Well, let's get looting. [BENDER WHISTLES]
jokes.setup: City has 72 hours before destruction
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate response is to start looting
jokes.explanation: Perfectly in-character response from Bender. His cheerful criminality in the face of apocalypse is darkly funny and memorable.

jokes.text: New Jersey was full.
jokes.setup: Listing places that were full of garbage: landfills, then...
jokes.punchline: New Jersey treated as equivalent to a landfill
jokes.explanation: Classic New Jersey insult delivered deadpan. Simple but effective regional humor that fits the documentary style.

jokes.text: Not even that really filthy one. You know the one I mean.
jokes.setup: No country would accept the garbage
jokes.punchline: Vague reference to 'filthy' country that audience must imagine
jokes.explanation: Clever way to make an international insult without targeting specific country. Engages audience to fill in the blank.

jokes.text: Some experts claim the ball may return to Earth. But their concerns were dismissed as 'depressing.'
jokes.setup: Scientists warned about the garbage returning
jokes.punchline: Valid concerns dismissed for being a downer
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how inconvenient truths get dismissed - feels very contemporary.

jokes.text: You got that off the Internet? In my day it was only used to download porn. -Actually, that's still true.
jokes.setup: Fry references how internet was used in his time for porn
jokes.punchline: Leela reveals nothing has changed in 1000 years
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about internet usage. The reveal that some things never change is well-timed.

jokes.text: Now that the garbage is in space... perhaps you can help me with my sexual inhibitions. -With gusto.
jokes.setup: Serious discussion about garbage crisis
jokes.punchline: Abrupt shift to sexual discussion, enthusiastically accepted
jokes.explanation: Classic non-sequitur humor. The abrupt topic change and enthusiastic response create good shock value.

jokes.text: -What do you do with it? -We recycle. -Robots are made from old beer cans. -And this can's made from old robots.
jokes.setup: Explanation of future recycling system
jokes.punchline: Circular logic of robots made from cans made from robots
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic about recycling. The circular nature of the recycling creates a perfect feedback loop of absurdity.

jokes.text: Give me a break. What do you do with it? We recycle.
jokes.setup: Fry defending 20th century garbage practices
jokes.punchline: Leela points out the obvious solution he's unaware of
jokes.explanation: Basic setup for the recycling explanation - functional but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Robots are made from old beer cans. And this can's made from old robots.
jokes.setup: Explanation of future recycling
jokes.punchline: Perfect circular economy between robots and beer cans
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic - robots made from beer cans holding beer cans made from robots. Perfect sci-fi absurdity.

jokes.text: That sandwich is made of discarded sandwiches.
jokes.setup: Continuing explanation of future recycling
jokes.punchline: Even food is recycled from the same food
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the recycling absurdity. The gross-out factor of recycled food adds to the humor.

jokes.text: The future is disgusting.
jokes.setup: Learning about future recycling practices
jokes.punchline: Fry's blunt assessment of the future he lives in
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry reaction. His blunt honesty about his new time period is very in-character and relatable.

jokes.text: -Those are from the 19th century. -They probably just copied us.
jokes.setup: Fry claims 20th century invented various items
jokes.punchline: When corrected, blames 19th century for copying the future
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Fry's illogical defensiveness and poor grasp of history. The backwards causality is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I hope this isn't a fraud like global warming or second-hand smoke.
jokes.setup: Mayor responding to garbage ball crisis
jokes.punchline: Dismisses real environmental/health issues as frauds
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire that works on multiple levels. The character's dismissal of real issues while facing fake crisis is darkly ironic.

jokes.text: That smell could be anything... a faulty stench coil, cheese on the lens, who knows?
jokes.setup: Wernstrom trying to discredit the Smell-O-Scope evidence
jokes.punchline: Made-up technical problems: 'stench coil,' 'cheese on the lens'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist pseudo-science. The made-up technical problems sound plausible enough to be funny rather than nonsensical.

jokes.text: The transmission ends there, sir. [RECORDED VOICE GAGS] No, I guess it keeps going. [RECORDED VOICE GAGS AND STOPS] There we go.
jokes.setup: Assistant announces transmission has ended
jokes.punchline: Transmission continues with more gagging, then actually ends
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective misdirection gag. The false ending followed by continuation is well-timed physical comedy.

jokes.text: -Do you mean him or me? -Him!
jokes.setup: Mayor calls 'the senile old man' right
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth asks for clarification, gets insulted
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective moment of character insecurity. Farnsworth's need for clarification backfires predictably.

jokes.text: -I'm glad we live here in Los Angeles. -Morbo agrees. [NEWSCASTERS LAUGH]
jokes.setup: News anchors commenting on New New York's crisis
jokes.punchline: LA anchors relieved they're safe, despite living in equally disaster-prone city
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about news anchors' perspective. The LA reference adds geographical humor for those familiar with disaster movies.

jokes.text: But suppose we sent a crew to plant an explosive on the fault line... between this mass of coffee grounds and this deposit of AOL floppy disks?
jokes.setup: Scientific plan to destroy garbage ball
jokes.punchline: Garbage ball composed of dated items: coffee grounds and AOL disks
jokes.explanation: Good period-specific humor with AOL floppy disks. References items that would resonate as dated waste for the original audience.

jokes.text: Oh, crap!
jokes.setup: Learning they must go on suicidal mission
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to terrible news
jokes.explanation: Simple understatement humor. The mild profanity in response to life-threatening mission is appropriately understated.

jokes.text: -Boo-hoo.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth explains billions would die if mission fails
jokes.punchline: Bender's sarcastic fake crying response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment. His callous indifference to mass death expressed through childish sarcasm is exactly in character.

jokes.text: Sporty.
jokes.setup: Anti-smell device activates with dramatic hiss
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual one-word assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of language. Fry's casual assessment of life-saving technology is very in-character understatement.

jokes.text: It's not filth. It's a monument to 20th-century achievements.
jokes.setup: Leela calls the garbage ball filth
jokes.punchline: Fry reframes garbage as achievements from his era
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's defensive pride in his era. The reframing of trash as monuments is cleverly absurd.

jokes.text: Eat my shorts. -Okay! Mmm, shorts!
jokes.setup: Bart Simpson doll says his catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Bender literally eats the shorts
jokes.explanation: Clever literal interpretation of famous catchphrase. Bender's robot literalism makes the impossible action plausible and funny.

jokes.text: This was garbage when it was new. Let's blow it up already.
jokes.setup: Looking at 20th-century consumer goods
jokes.punchline: Items were worthless even when they were new
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about consumer culture. The dismissal of nostalgic items as always worthless is sharply delivered.

jokes.text: There's your problem. The professor put the counter on upside down.
jokes.setup: Bomb timer showing confusing time
jokes.punchline: Timer was installed upside down, showing wrong time
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that's both logical and absurd. Farnsworth's incompetence creates genuine danger through simple mistake.

jokes.text: That idiot! It wasn't set for 25 minutes, it was set for 52 seconds.
jokes.setup: Discovering the timer error
jokes.punchline: They have seconds instead of minutes before explosion
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation that ramps up tension. The dramatic reduction in time creates genuine peril from comedy setup.

jokes.text: Okay, keep your space pants on. I'll take care of this.
jokes.setup: Crisis situation with explosion
jokes.punchline: Bender uses future version of 'keep your pants on'
jokes.explanation: Good future-speak adaptation of common phrase - 'space pants' updates the idiom.

jokes.text: Hey! You'll put somebody's eye out! Okay, keep your space pants on. I'll take care of this.
jokes.setup: Explosion launching debris everywhere
jokes.punchline: Bender treats flying garbage like overreaction, mentions 'space pants'
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment showing his casual attitude toward danger. 'Space pants' is a nice future-world detail.

jokes.text: All in all, this is one day Mittens the Kitten won't soon forget.
jokes.setup: News report about garbage crisis
jokes.punchline: Suddenly references random kitten as if it's relevant
jokes.explanation: Classic non-sequitur news humor. The random kitten reference is perfectly absurd and unexpected.

jokes.text: Kittens give Morbo gas.
jokes.setup: Previous kitten reference
jokes.punchline: Alien news anchor's personal digestive information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Morbo character moment. His casual sharing of personal alien biology while presenting news is wonderfully bizarre.

jokes.text: And when my toilet broke, I just went straight in the garbage can.
jokes.setup: Fry admitting to wasteful 20th-century habits
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to using garbage can as toilet
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry oversharing moment. His casual admission of disgusting behavior is perfectly in-character and cringe-funny.

jokes.text: Lab access and five grad students, at least three Chinese.
jokes.setup: Wernstrom negotiating payment for saving city
jokes.punchline: Specific ethnic requirement for graduate students
jokes.explanation: Observational humor about academic stereotypes. The specificity of the demand adds character detail but relies on ethnic stereotypes.

jokes.text: What plan? I'm set for life. Au revoir, suckers.
jokes.setup: Mayor asks for Wernstrom's plan to save the city
jokes.punchline: Reveals he has no plan, was just scamming for benefits
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and character reveal. Wernstrom's complete lack of heroism and shameless scamming is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: -That rat! Do something! -I would, but he's got tenure.
jokes.setup: Wernstrom abandons city after getting university benefits
jokes.punchline: Can't fire him because he has academic tenure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant academic satire. The idea that tenure protects even city-betraying scientists is perfectly absurd and well-delivered.

jokes.text: Stephanie, cancel the maid. Have her come tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Preparing for apocalypse by taking action
jokes.punchline: Contradictory instruction to cancel then reschedule maid
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing privileged disconnect from crisis. The contradictory instruction shows confused priorities.

jokes.text: Good Lord! A second ball of garbage! That just might work!
jokes.setup: Fry suggests using garbage to fight garbage
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth treats this as brilliant scientific insight
jokes.explanation: Good moment of treating simple idea as genius. Farnsworth's excitement over obvious solution is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You can't just find garbage in the streets. City's been garbage-free for 500 years!
jokes.setup: Need to create garbage ball to save city
jokes.punchline: Reveals future's cleanliness creates crisis inability to make garbage
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic reversal. The future's environmental progress becomes a handicap when they need to create garbage. Great world-building humor.

jokes.text: This Slurm can? Now it's garbage. These papers? Garbage. This picture of your wife? Pure garbage.
jokes.setup: Fry teaching people how to make garbage
jokes.punchline: Escalates from objects to insulting someone's wife photo
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from literal to personal. Fry's tactless progression from objects to insulting family photos is perfectly in-character.

jokes.text: Get that robot some more beer!
jokes.setup: Teaching garbage-making, Bender drinking beer
jokes.punchline: More beer means more recyclable containers to throw away
jokes.explanation: Nice integration of Bender's alcoholism into the solution. Using his vice as a garbage-production method is clever character work.

jokes.text: . . .three, four, five, six. . . Just fire the damn thing.
jokes.setup: Countdown for launch going wrong direction
jokes.punchline: Frustrated order to just fire despite bad countdown
jokes.explanation: Good moment of scientific procedure breakdown. The frustrated abandonment of proper countdown protocol is relatable and funny.

jokes.text: I'll get you, Farnsworth, even if it takes another 100 years!
jokes.setup: Wernstrom defeated and humiliated again
jokes.punchline: Threatens another century-long revenge plot
jokes.explanation: Good callback that establishes the cyclical nature of their rivalry. The threat of another century-long plot is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Should we be celebrating? What if it returns to Earth like the first one? -Who cares? That would take centuries. -Exactly. It's none of our concern. -That's the 20th-century spirit!
jokes.setup: Leela raises concern about second garbage ball returning
jokes.punchline: Everyone dismisses long-term consequences, Fry celebrates this as 20th-century thinking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant thematic conclusion. The future people adopting 20th-century short-term thinking completes the episode's circle perfectly. Fry's pride in this attitude is the perfect capstone.

jokes.text: Who cares? That would take centuries. Exactly. It's none of our concern. That's the 20th-century spirit!
jokes.setup: Concern about future consequences of their solution
jokes.punchline: Dismissing future problems as classic 20th-century thinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect thematic conclusion - the characters learn nothing and repeat the exact same short-sighted thinking that created the problem. Excellent environmental satire.

tmdb_overview: A big piece of garbage that was released a thousand years ago is now on the way to Earth to destroy it. After Fry, Leela and Bender fail on placing a bomb on it, the city has to build another big piece of garbage, yet that may be a problem since trash doesn't exist in year 3000.
